help.intro: Here you'll find answers to the most frequently asked questions about how Sevillaneando works.
help.createEventBody:
When creating an event you can choose whether it is public or private.

• Public events are sent for moderation before appearing in the app. Once approved, any user can view them and sign up.

• Private events are only for you and the people you share the link with. They don't go through moderation and are not visible on the map or in the general listing.

The start and end date is optional. If not set, the event appears as "Undefined".
help.priceBody:
When creating or editing an event you must indicate the price. There are two options, not both at once:

• Fixed price: a single value in €. Set 0 if the event is free.

• Price range: a minimum and a maximum (e.g., tickets from €10 to €25). Both fields are required if you use this option.
help.recurrenceBody:
When creating an event you can indicate if it repeats: daily, weekly, biweekly or monthly.

If you enable recurrence, you can also set an end date until which the event repeats. Without an end date, recurrence is indefinite.

Recurring events are shown as a single event in the app; recurrence is informational only.
help.privateBody:
When you create a private event, the app generates a unique access link.

You can share that link with whoever you want. Whoever opens it will be able to view the event and sign up.

The link can be copied from the event detail screen, using the "Share private link" button.

Events you've joined via link appear in the menu → "Attending events".
help.moderationBody:
Public events go through a moderation process before being published.

• A moderator can approve or reject them from "Approve events" in the menu.

• Once approved, the event appears on the map and listing for all users.

• Moderators can also edit the data of any already approved public event.

• Private events don't go through moderation and are not visible or editable by moderators.
help.saveBody:
You can save any public event by tapping the bookmark icon on the detail screen.

Saved events appear in the menu → "Saved events". This lets you keep a list of events of interest without having to sign up.
help.attendBody:
You can sign up for a public event from its detail screen. Events you're attending appear in the menu → "Attending events".

You can also view all your attending events in calendar format from the menu → "Calendar". Days with events are marked and you can tap any day to see the events for that date.
help.calendarBody:
The calendar shows all events you're attending organised by day.

Days with events are marked. Tapping a day shows the list of events for that date. You can tap any event to see its detail.
help.mapBody:
From the menu → "Events map" you can see all approved public events located on the Seville map.

You can filter by category and by distance radius from your location. Tapping a marker shows a summary of the event; tap again to see the full detail.
help.categoriesBody:
Categories are used to classify events. You can filter by category from the main screen and the map.

Administrators can create, edit and delete categories from the menu → "Manage categories". If a category is deleted, events that had it assigned automatically move to the "Others" category.
help.routesBody:
The app can suggest a tourist route around Seville based on your interests and availability.

From the menu → "Routes" you can view saved routes or request a new recommendation. The route is shown on the map with points of interest in order.

The interests you indicate in your profile influence the routes suggested to you.
help.profileBody:
From the menu you can edit your profile: name, photo and list of interests.

Interests are used to personalise recommended routes. You can add as many as you like (music, gastronomy, history, etc.).

You can also change your password from the profile. If you want to delete your account, the option is at the bottom of the profile editing screen.
help.friendsBody:
You can follow other users from their profile. If two users follow each other they are considered "friends".

From the menu → "Friends" you can see your followers, who you follow, your friends and search for new users by name.

You can send private messages to any user from their profile.
help.rateBody:
Once a finished event you were attending, you can leave a rating with a score and comment from the event detail screen.

Ratings are visible to all users. An event can only be rated once.
help.chatBody:
Every public event has a real-time chat visible on its detail screen.

Any user can participate in the event chat. You can send text and images.
help.contactBody:
Have a suggestion for improvement or want to know the status of an event you submitted for moderation?

You can contact the app team directly through the internal chat:

• Admin Demo
• Moderator Demo

Find them under "Friends" → search by name and send them a private message.

legal.title: Licenses & attributions
legal.osmDesc: This app uses OpenStreetMap data and must maintain visible attribution on map screens.
legal.nominatimDesc: Nominatim is used for address searches and reverse geocoding.
legal.rnDesc: This app is built with React Native and Expo, distributed under the MIT licence.
legal.nestDesc: The server for this application is developed with NestJS, distributed under the MIT licence.
legal.typeormDesc: The server data access layer uses TypeORM, distributed under the MIT licence.
legal.firebaseDesc: User authentication is managed through Firebase (Google LLC), subject to Firebase Terms of Service.
legal.cloudinaryDesc: App images are stored and served through Cloudinary, subject to their Terms of Service.
legal.ticketmasterDesc: Some events are obtained through the Ticketmaster API, subject to their Terms of Use.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.section1Body: Sevillaneando is an academic application developed as a Final Degree Project (TFG) at the University of Seville. The collected data is processed solely for demonstrative and academic purposes.
privacy.section2Intro: When you register and use the application we may process the following personal data:
privacy.section2Body:
• Name and email address (registration)
• Profile photo (optional)
• Geographic location (optional, for nearby event search)
• Selected cultural interests
• Generated content: events, routes, reviews and chat messages
privacy.section3Body:
We process your data to:
• Manage your account and authentication
• Show you relevant cultural events in Seville
• Allow you to create and share routes and reviews
• Facilitate communication between users (chat)
privacy.section3Legal: The legal basis for processing is your express consent (Art. 6.1.a GDPR), given by accepting this policy during registration.
privacy.section4Body:
To provide the service we use the following external providers, each with their own privacy policy:
• Firebase (Google LLC) — authentication and file storage
• Cloudinary — image hosting
• Ticketmaster — public cultural event data
• Google Gemini — event information enrichment
• OpenStreetMap / Nominatim — maps and geocoding
privacy.section5Body: Your data is retained while your account is active. When you delete your account, your profile data, photo and activity history are deleted immediately. Private messages where neither party has an active account are permanently deleted within a maximum of 30 days.
privacy.section6Body:
Under the GDPR (EU Regulation 2016/679) you have the right to:
• Access your personal data
• Rectify inaccurate data
• Erase your data (right to be forgotten)
• Data portability
• Withdraw consent at any time
privacy.section6Footer: You can exercise these rights by deleting your account from the app profile or by contacting the project manager.
privacy.section7Body: We apply technical measures to protect your data: encrypted communications via HTTPS/TLS, authentication via Firebase, input validation and request rate limiting.
